name: Santander and Costa Quebrada Loop
name_original: El Catedral, Centro Botín, Parque Natural de Las Dunas de Liencres, Costa Quebrada – vuelta por Santander
description: Cycle 26.6 miles from Santander's landmarks to the rugged Costa Quebrada and Liencres Dunes Natural Park on this difficult route.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Costera offer diverse terrain, from coastal paths to greenways and more challenging inland ascents. The region features a mix of flat, scenic routes along the coast and river valleys, alongside areas with moderate elevation changes. Cyclists can expect varied landscapes, including stretches near the Cantabrian Sea and routes through rural areas. Best touring cycling routes around Costera The most popular touring cycling route is Santander Coastal Trail, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail that takes about 52 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the coastline, offering views of the sea. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Suances to Santillana del Mar – circular via the Besaya Greenway and the coast, a moderate 20.2 miles (32.5 km) path. This route combines a greenway with coastal sections, passing through varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the El Faro de Suances y la Playa de Los Locos to the Estatua de Francisco de Asís – round trip via the Vía Verde del Besaya, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and a greenway, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Costera is defined by coastal trails, greenways, and routes through rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging routes with greater elevation gain. The routes in Costera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 1,300 reviews. More than 15,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Costera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Costera?
A: There are nearly a thousand touring cycling routes available in Costera, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy coastal rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Costera?
A: Costera offers diverse terrain for touring cyclists. You can expect flat, scenic routes along the coast and river valleys, as well as greenways. Inland, the landscape features mountains and valleys, with some routes providing more challenging ascents through areas like the Serra Grossa. You'll also encounter picturesque orange groves and Mediterranean rainforests.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Costera?
A: Yes, Costera has over 100 easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. Many of these follow greenways or flat coastal paths, providing a relaxed and safe environment. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018643881/santander-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for a leisurely family ride.

Q: What are some scenic touring cycling routes with great viewpoints in Costera?
A: For scenic rides and viewpoints, consider routes that hug the coast or venture into the mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012455969/suances-to-santillana-del-mar-circular-via-the-besaya-greenway-and-the-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suances to Santillana del Mar – circular via the Besaya Greenway and the coast&lt;/a&gt; offers varied scenery, combining greenway sections with coastal views. Routes through the mountains and valleys of La Costera also provide stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Costera?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Costera are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451006/pas-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012281340/santona-forts-and-marshes-loop-from-noja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Forts and Marshes Loop from Noja&lt;/a&gt;, which offer diverse landscapes without needing a return trip.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit along touring cycling routes in Costera?
A: Costera is rich in history and culture. You can explore towns like Xàtiva with its imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3828681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Palace&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobrellano Palace&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features ancient castles such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venero Tower&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6656175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle and Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, many of which are accessible from various cycling paths.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural features to see while cycling in Costera?
A: Absolutely! Costera boasts beautiful natural features. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suscuaja River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8022536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Scenic Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the unique Mediterranean rainforest of La Cabrentà d'Estubeny with its caves and geological formations, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguanaz River and Frenchman’s Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Costera?
A: The pleasant climate of La Costera, particularly in the Valencia region, makes it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for multi-day tours, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter, though routes are available year-round.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Costera?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Costera, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal trails to greenways and rural landscapes, and the variety of routes suitable for all abilities. The well-equipped starting and ending points with services for cyclists are also frequently appreciated.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Costera for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Costera offers over 500 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and traverse mountainous terrain, such as the Serra Grossa. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012252332/el-capricho-de-gaudi-the-abadia-cisterciense-de-santa-maria-y-santa-maria-de-los-angeles-return-from-san-vicente-de-la-barquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Capricho de Gaudi, the Abadia Cisterciense de Santa Maria y Santa Maria de los Angeles - return from San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017346946/laredo-and-santuario-de-la-bien-aparecida-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laredo and Santuario de la Bien Aparecida Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find accommodation and places to eat along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the multi-stage cycling tours in La Costera are designed with cyclists in mind. Each starting and ending point, as well as many towns along the routes, provides essential services. You'll find accommodation, restaurants, cafes, and places to resupply with water and food, ensuring a comfortable touring experience.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to historic towns or cultural sites?
A: Many routes in Costera allow you to explore historic towns and cultural sites. For instance, the region includes municipalities like Canals, which is part of the Borgia Route, and Xàtiva with its famous castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017302055/san-vicente-de-la-barquera-to-comillas-circular-through-the-oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera to Comillas – circular through the Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; route connects two charming coastal towns with rich heritage.
tips:
The Cathedral of Our Lady of the Assumption (Santander) is located very close to the sea and its appearance is very austere if we compare it with others of the time.

The history of the cathedral dates back to the times when Santander was granted the status of jurisdiction in 1187, under the tutelage of the abbot of its church, this meant a great economic boost for the town with which the cathedral could be built and the castle, which was later called Puebla Vieja, as well as walls that defended the busy port and its inhabitants.

The current building of the Cathedral of Santander is made up of 2 overlapping Gothic-style churches. The lower one was built in the first third of the s. XIII is the parish of Christ and the upper one was built during that century and has had to be rebuilt and enlarged, in 1941 Santander suffered a fire that affected this part of the Cathedral.

A must-see tourist attraction in Santander. The building, designed by Renzo Piano, fits perfectly with its surroundings and there are excellent exhibitions (fixed and temporary) of fine art. Enjoy the beautiful views from the roof terrace and have a coffee or a snack in the cafeteria. You can buy tickets online at www.centrobotin.org.

The beach and dunes of the natural park are an excellent place to take a well-deserved break. If you like, you can also walk around in the shady forest. You can stock up on all kinds of provisions at the beach parking lot.

One of the most spectacular, diverse and rich stretches of the Iberian Peninsula. It extends for 20 kilometers of coastline from Santander to Miengo. Enjoy the beautiful coves and beaches with crystal clear and turquoise waters.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The full route, covering approximately 42.8 km (26.6 miles), is estimated to take around 2 hours and 55 minutes by touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your pace, stops at attractions, and fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse cycling experience. Within Santander, the terrain is generally flat with dedicated bike lanes, making it easy. As you head towards the Liencres Dunes Natural Park and Costa Quebrada, the terrain becomes more challenging. Expect significant climbs, including sections with gradients up to 30% near the cliffs of Portio. Some parts may be unpaved, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the Santander city sections are easy and suitable for all fitness levels, the overall route is considered difficult due to demanding sections in the Liencres Dunes Natural Park and along the Costa Quebrada. These areas feature significant climbs and potentially unpaved paths, requiring good to very good fitness. Beginners might consider cycling only the urban parts or using an e-bike for the more challenging sections.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route combines cultural landmarks with stunning natural beauty. You'll pass by the historic Cathedral of Our Lady of the Assumption (Santander) and the modern Centro Botín in Santander. Further along, you'll experience the unique dune system of the Liencres Dunes Natural Park and the dramatic cliffs, coves, and geological formations of the Costa Quebrada.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is milder and more stable. Summers can be warm, especially in urban areas, but the coastal breeze can be refreshing. Winters can be wet and windy, making some exposed coastal sections less enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this route starts and ends in Santander, you can find various parking options within the city. Consider public parking garages near the city center or the port area, which offer convenient access to the initial landmarks like the Cathedral and Centro Botín. For specific parking details, it's advisable to check local Santander parking information.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling through the Parque Natural de Las Dunas de Liencres?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the Parque Natural de Las Dunas de Liencres. However, as a natural park, it's essential to respect all environmental regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife or vegetation. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in many outdoor areas in Cantabria, specific regulations may apply within the Parque Natural de Las Dunas de Liencres and on certain beaches. It's best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, it follows parts of the Senda del Litoral and the Northern Route (Cantabria). Within Santander, you'll also find it uses segments of paths like the Circuito de hierba de La Remonta and various city bike lanes, such as those near Plaza Emilio Botín and Muelle de Albareda.

faqs.question: What makes this cycling route unique or special?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its exceptional blend of experiences. It seamlessly combines the urban charm and cultural landmarks of Santander with the wild, breathtaking coastal scenery of the Costa Quebrada and the unique ecosystems of the Liencres Dunes Natural Park. It's a 'museum of nature' offering diverse landscapes, from cityscapes to dramatic cliffs and pristine beaches, all in one varied cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to eat along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and bars in Santander, especially near the Cathedral, Centro Botín, and along the promenade. As you venture out towards the Liencres Dunes Natural Park and Costa Quebrada, options become sparser, so it's advisable to carry snacks and water, or plan stops in smaller coastal towns if you deviate slightly from the main path.

faqs.question: Can I rent an e-bike for this route in Santander?
faqs.answer: Yes, e-bike rentals are available in Santander. Opting for an e-bike can make the more challenging, hilly sections through the Liencres Dunes Natural Park and along the Costa Quebrada more accessible and enjoyable, allowing you to fully appreciate the stunning scenery without excessive physical strain.

waypoints.name: Cathedral of Our Lady of the Assumption (Santander)
